lines.gurmukhi: ਦਹ ਦਿਸ ਬੂਡੀ ਪਵਨੁ ਝੁਲਾਵੈ ਡੋਰਿ ਰਹੀ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: deh dis booddee pavan jhulaavai ddor rahee liv laaee |3|
lines.english: The drowning person is blown around in the ten directions by the wind, but I hold tight to the string of the Lord's Love. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਨਮਨਿ ਮਨੂਆ ਸੁੰਨਿ ਸਮਾਨਾ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਭਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: aunaman manooaa sun samaanaa dubidhaa duramat bhaagee |
lines.english: The disturbed mind has been absorbed in the Lord; duality and evil-mindedness have run away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਅਨਭਉ ਇਕੁ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ॥੪॥੨॥੪੬॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer anbhau ik dekhiaa raam naam liv laagee |4|2|46|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I have seen the One Lord, the Fearless One; I am attuned to the Name of the Lord. ||4||2||46||

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਲਟਤ ਪਵਨ ਚਕ੍ਰ ਖਟੁ ਭੇਦੇ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੁੰਨ ਅਨਰਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: aulattat pavan chakr khatt bhede surat sun anaraagee |
lines.english: I turned my breath inwards, and pierced through the six chakras of the body, and my awareness was centered on the Primal Void of the Absolute Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉੜੀ ਬੈਰਾਗਣਿ ਤਿਪਦੇ₃ ॥
lines.translit: gaurree bairaagan tipade₃ |
lines.english: Gauree Bairaagan, Thi-Padhay:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨ ਮਨ ਹੀ ਉਲਟਿ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: mere man man hee ulatt samaanaa |
lines.english: My mind has turned away from the world, and is absorbed in the Mind of God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵੈ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਮਰੈ ਨ ਜੀਵੈ ਤਾਸੁ ਖੋਜੁ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aavai na jaae marai na jeevai taas khoj bairaagee |1|
lines.english: Search for the One who does not come or go, who does not die and is not born, O renunciate. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਅਕਲਿ ਭਈ ਅਵਰੈ ਨਾਤਰੁ ਥਾ ਬੇਗਾਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad akal bhee avarai naatar thaa begaanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, my understanding has been changed; otherwise, I was totally ignorant. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਵਰੈ ਦੂਰਿ ਦੂਰਿ ਫੁਨਿ ਨਿਵਰੈ ਜਿਨਿ ਜੈਸਾ ਕਰਿ ਮਾਨਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: nivarai door door fun nivarai jin jaisaa kar maaniaa |
lines.english: That which was near has become distant, and again, that which was distant is near, for those who realize the Lord as He is.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਲਉਤੀ ਕਾ ਜੈਸੇ ਭਇਆ ਬਰੇਡਾ ਜਿਨਿ ਪੀਆ ਤਿਨਿ ਜਾਨਿਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: alautee kaa jaise bheaa bareddaa jin peea tin jaaniaa |2|
lines.english: It is like the sugar water made from the candy; only one who drinks it knows its taste. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੀ ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਕਥਾ ਕਾਇ ਸਿਉ ਕਹੀਐ ਐਸਾ ਕੋਇ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: teree niragun kathaa kaae siau kaheeai aisaa koe bibekee |
lines.english: Unto whom should I speak Your speech, O Lord; it is beyond the three qualities. Is there anyone with such discerning wisdom?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਜਿਨਿ ਦੀਆ ਪਲੀਤਾ ਤਿਨਿ ਤੈਸੀ ਝਲ ਦੇਖੀ ॥੩॥੩॥੪੭॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer jin deea paleetaa tin taisee jhal dekhee |3|3|47|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, as is the fuse which you apply, so is the flash you will see. ||3||3||47||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: gaurree |
lines.english: Gauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਹ ਪਾਵਸ ਸਿੰਧੁ ਧੂਪ ਨਹੀ ਛਹੀਆ ਤਹ ਉਤਪਤਿ ਪਰਲਉ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: teh paavas sindh dhoop nahee chhaheea teh utapat parlau naahee |
lines.english: There is no rainy season, ocean, sunshine or shade, no creation or destruction there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਵਨ ਮਿਰਤੁ ਨ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਬਿਆਪੈ ਸੁੰਨ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਦੋਊ ਤਹ ਨਾਹੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jeevan mirat na dukh sukh biaapai sun samaadh doaoo teh naahee |1|
lines.english: No life or death, no pain or pleasure is felt there. There is only the Primal Trance of Samaadhi, and no duality. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਲਿ ਨਹੀ ਚਢੈ ਜਾਇ ਨ ਮੁਕਾਤੀ ਹਲੁਕੀ ਲਗੈ ਨ ਭਾਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tul nahee chadtai jaae na mukaatee halukee lagai na bhaaree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: It is not measured, and it is not exhausted. It is neither light nor heavy. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜ ਕੀ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਹੈ ਨਿਰਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: sehaj kee akath kathaa hai niraaree |
lines.english: The description of the state of intuitive poise is indescribable and sublime.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਰਧ ਉਰਧ ਦੋਊ ਤਹ ਨਾਹੀ ਰਾਤਿ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਤਹ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: aradh uradh doaoo teh naahee raat dinas teh naahee |
lines.english: Neither lower nor upper worlds are there; neither day nor night are there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਮ ਅਗੋਚਰੁ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਲਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: agam agochar rahai nirantar gur kirapaa te laheeai |
lines.english: The Inaccessible and Unfathomable Lord dwells there within Himself; by Guru's Grace, He is found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲੁ ਨਹੀ ਪਵਨੁ ਪਾਵਕੁ ਫੁਨਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤਹਾ ਸਮਾਹੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jal nahee pavan paavak fun naahee satigur tahaa samaahee |2|
lines.english: There is no water, wind or fire; there, the True Guru is contained. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਉ ਗੁਰ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਸਤਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹੀਐ ॥੩॥੪॥੪੮॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer bal jaau gur apune satasangat mil raheeai |3|4|48|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I am a sacrifice to my Guru; I remain in the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy. ||3||4||48||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਪੁ ਪੁੰਨੁ ਦੁਇ ਬੈਲ ਬਿਸਾਹੇ ਪਵਨੁ ਪੂਜੀ ਪਰਗਾਸਿਓ ॥
lines.translit: paap pun due bail bisaahe pavan poojee paragaasio |
lines.english: With both sin and virtue, the ox of the body is purchased; the air of the breath is the capital which has appeared.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉੜੀ₂ ॥
lines.translit: gaurree₂ |
lines.english: Gauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਗੂਣਿ ਭਰੀ ਘਟ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਇਨ ਬਿਧਿ ਟਾਂਡ ਬਿਸਾਹਿਓ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: trisanaa goon bharee ghatt bheetar in bidh ttaandd bisaahio |1|
lines.english: The bag on its back is filled with desire; this is how we purchase the herd. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਕੀਓ ਬਨਜਾਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sagal sansaar keeo banajaaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He has made the whole world his peddler. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸਾ ਨਾਇਕੁ ਰਾਮੁ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: aisaa naaeik raam hamaaraa |
lines.english: My Lord is such a wealthy merchant!

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਤਤੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਦਾਨੁ ਨਿਬੇਰਹਿ ਟਾਂਡਾ ਉਤਰਿਓ ਪਾਰਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: panch tat mil daan nibereh ttaanddaa utario paaraa |2|
lines.english: The five elements join together and divide up their loot. This is how our herd is disposed of! ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਦੁਇ ਭਏ ਜਗਾਤੀ ਮਨ ਤਰੰਗ ਬਟਵਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh due bhe jagaatee man tarang battavaaraa |
lines.english: Sexual desire and anger are the tax-collectors, and the waves of the mind are the highway robbers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਾਟੀ ਚਢਤ ਬੈਲੁ ਇਕੁ ਥਾਕਾ ਚਲੋ ਗੋਨਿ ਛਿਟਕਾਈ ॥੩॥੫॥੪੯॥
lines.translit: ghaattee chadtat bail ik thaakaa chalo gon chhittakaaee |3|5|49|
lines.english: Going uphill, the ox has grown weary; throwing off his load, he continues on his journey. ||3||5||49||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਕਬੀਰੁ ਸੁਨਹੁ ਰੇ ਸੰਤਹੁ ਅਬ ਐਸੀ ਬਨਿ ਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: kehat kabeer sunahu re santahu ab aisee ban aaee |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, listen, O Saints: This is the state of affairs now!

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉੜੀ ਪੰਚਪਦਾ₃ ॥
lines.translit: gaurree panchapadaa₃ |
lines.english: Gauree, Panch-Padhay:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੇਵਕੜੈ ਦਿਨ ਚਾਰਿ ਹੈ ਸਾਹੁਰੜੈ ਜਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: pevakarrai din chaar hai saahurarrai jaanaa |
lines.english: For a few short days, the soul-bride stays in her parent's house; then, she must go to her in-laws.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧਾ ਲੋਕੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਈ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਏਆਣਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: andhaa lok na jaanee moorakh eaanaa |1|
lines.english: The blind, foolish and ignorant people do not know this. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਡਡੀਆ ਬਾਧੈ ਧਨ ਖੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: kahu ddaddeea baadhai dhan kharree |
lines.english: Tell me, why is the bride wearing her ordinary clothes?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਹੂ ਘਰਿ ਆਏ ਮੁਕਲਾਊ ਆਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: paahoo ghar aae mukalaaoo aae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The guests have arrived at her home, and her Husband has come to take her away. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਹ ਜਿ ਦਿਸੈ ਖੂਹੜੀ ਕਉਨ ਲਾਜੁ ਵਹਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: oeh ji disai khooharree kaun laaj vahaaree |
lines.english: Who has lowered the rope of the breath down, into the well of the world which we see?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਅਪੁਨਾ ਕਾਰਜੁ ਸਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: saahib hoe deaal kripaa kare apunaa kaaraj savaare |
lines.english: When the Lord and Master is kind and grants His Grace, then her affairs are all resolved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਾਜੁ ਘੜੀ ਸਿਉ ਤੂਟਿ ਪੜੀ ਉਠਿ ਚਲੀ ਪਨਿਹਾਰੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: laaj gharree siau toott parree utth chalee panihaaree |2|
lines.english: The rope of the breath breaks away from the pitcher of the body, and the water-carrier gets up and departs. ||2||
